description: A vertical designed public-service/promotional graphic. The background is a color photograph of an active construction/excavation site—a long dug trench in brown earth, with a yellow excavator arm and bucket prominent at the left edge and blurred workers in high-visibility vests and equipment in the soft-focus background. A semi-transparent dark blue/purple gradient overlay darkens the image for text legibility. Centered is the large white line-art seal of the U.S. Department of Labor ('Department of Labor / United States of America' with the eagle, anvil and shield). Below, in white italic serif type: 'Have a question about your rights at work? We can help.' followed by a large phone number '1-866-487-2365' and, at lower right, 'dol.gov'. The design is clean, official and service-oriented rather than overtly political. The messaging technique positions the federal Labor Department as an accessible helper/protector of workers' rights, providing a concrete call-to-action (phone number and website). Target audience: American workers who may need help with workplace issues.
notable_text: Have a question about your rights at work? We can help. 1-866-487-2365 dol.gov
